- example-skill: Skill: example-skill
- odoo-18: Master index for Odoo 18 guides. This file provides a quick reference to find the appropriate detailed guide for each topic. Use this as an index to locate specific guides when working with Odoo 18 code. - Finding the appropriate guide for an Odoo 18 task,Understanding module structure and conventions,Looking up best practices for specific Odoo features
- brain-dump: /brain-dump - ADHD Brain Processor
- checkpoint: Checkpoint (GSD Phase Transition)
- end-work: End Work Session
- implement: /implement - Context-Engineered Implementation Phase
- learn: Learn - Capture Knowledge
- plan: /plan - Context-Engineered Planning Phase
- pulse: Pulse Check (Daily Work Rhythm)
- quick-commit: Quick Commit
- README: Commands
- reminder: /reminder - In-Session Action Capture
- research: /research - Context-Engineered Discovery Phase
- start-work: Start Work Session
- status: Status Check
- validate-work: Validate Work
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

173	    def _crm_milestone_line_missed(self, dt, done, na):
174	        self.ensure_one()
175	        if not dt or done or na:
176	            return False
177	        today = fields.Date.context_today(self)
178	        line_day = fields.Datetime.context_timestamp(self, dt).date()
179	        return line_day < today

file.content:
---
name: Docker Odoo workflow for ACG project
description: Project folder structure, Docker commands, and workflow for Odoo development across all project directories
type: project
originSessionId: 88ea4c87-1f4e-4765-b448-f79f0049550c
---
Docker workflow for Odoo development in the ACG project.

**Why:** The Odoo instance runs in Docker containers, and all debugging/upgrades must go through Docker commands. Database is 'odoo18', not 'postgres'. User works across multiple project folders and needs consistent commands regardless of current directory.

**How to apply:**

## Project Folder Structure
- **Custom Addons**: `X:\03_ACG\Custom_Addons` — Odoo custom modules (search_leads, crm_custom_fields, etc.)
- **Docker Config**: `X:\03_ACG\Odoo_Docker` — Docker Compose files and container configuration
- **Documentation**: `X:\03_ACG\Odoo_Docs` — Project documentation and guides

## Container Names
- Odoo container: `odoo_docker-web-1`
- Database container: `odoo_docker-db-1`
- Nginx container: `odoo_docker-nginx-1`

## Database
- Database name: `odoo18` (NOT postgres!)
- User: `odoo`
- Connection: `psql -U odoo -d odoo18`

## Common Commands

### Upgrade Module
```bash
docker exec odoo_docker-web-1 odoo -d odoo18 -u module_name --stop-after-init
```

### Restart Odoo
```bash
cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker
docker-compose restart web
```

### Check Database Tables
```bash
docker exec odoo_docker-db-1 psql -U odoo -d odoo18 -c "SELECT ..."
```

### View Logs
```bash
docker logs odoo_docker-web-1 --tail=50
```

### Full Restart (if needed)
```bash
cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker
docker-compose down
docker-compose up -d
```

### Restart Nginx (for 502 errors)
```bash
docker restart odoo_docker-nginx-1
```

## File Paths
- Custom addons: `X:/03_ACG/Custom_Addons` (mounted to `/mnt/extra-addons` in container)
- Config: `/etc/odoo/odoo.conf` (inside container)

## Access Points
- Direct Odoo: http://localhost:8080
- Via Nginx HTTPS: https://odoo-dev.local:8443

## Typical Workflow
1. Edit code in `X:/03_ACG/Custom_Addons/module_name/`
2. Upgrade module: `docker exec odoo_docker-web-1 odoo -d odoo18 -u module_name --stop-after-init`
3. Restart: `cd X:/03_ACG/Odoo_Docker && docker-compose restart web`
4. Test in browser (clear cache: Ctrl+Shift+R)

from odoo import api, fields, models, _
from odoo.exceptions import AccessError, UserError


class DocumentLibraryItem(models.Model):
    _name = "document.library.item"
    _description = "Document Library Item"
    _inherit = ["mail.thread", "mail.activity.mixin"]
    _order = "sequence, name"

    active = fields.Boolean(default=True)
    sequence = fields.Integer(default=10)

    name = fields.Char(required=True, tracking=True)
    description = fields.Html()

    # SharePoint link + identity fields (always available, regardless of mode)
    sharepoint_url = fields.Char(string="SharePoint URL", required=True, tracking=True)
    sharepoint_path = fields.Char(string="SharePoint Path / Folder")

    sync_target_id = fields.Many2one(
        "document.library.sync.target",
        string="Sync target",
        ondelete="set null",
        index=True,
        readonly=True,
        help="Sync configuration that pulled this file from SharePoint (if applicable).",
    )

    sp_site_id = fields.Char(string="SP Site ID", index=True)
    sp_drive_id = fields.Char(string="SP Drive ID", index=True)
    sp_item_id = fields.Char(string="SP Item ID", index=True)
    sp_list_id = fields.Char(string="SP List ID", index=True)
    sp_list_item_id = fields.Char(string="SP List Item ID", index=True)

    # Link to departments
    department_ids = fields.Many2many(
        "hr.department",
        "document_library_item_department_rel",
        "library_item_id",
        "department_id",
        string="Departments",
    )

    owner_id = fields.Many2one(
        "res.users",
        string="Owner",
        default=lambda self: self.env.user,
        index=True,
        tracking=True,
    )

    tag_ids = fields.Many2many("document.library.tag", string="Tags")

    related_document_ids = fields.Many2many(
        "document.library.item",
        "document_library_item_relation",
        "document_id",
        "related_document_id",
        string="Related Documents",
        help="Link this document to other related documents"
    )

    doc_type = fields.Selection(
        [
            ("guideline", "Guideline"),
            ("policy", "Policy"),
            ("procedure", "Procedure"),
            ("form", "Form / Template"),
            ("media", "Media Resource"),
            ("template", "Template"),
            ("standard", "Standard & Regulations"),
            ("work_instruction", "Work Instruction"),
            ("evidence", "Evidence"),
            ("other", "Other"),
        ],
        default="other",
        string="Document Type",
        index=True,
        tracking=True,
    )

    # Global toggle flag (computed)
    revision_control_enabled = fields.Boolean(
        compute="_compute_revision_control_enabled",
        store=False,
    )

    def action_open_sharepoint(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        if not self.sharepoint_url:
            return False

        return {
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_url',
            'url': self.sharepoint_url,
            'target': 'new',
        }

    @api.depends_context("uid")

**Model changes (company_information.py):**

- Added `document_library_ids` field to link documents from the document_library module

- Added `document_library_count` computed field to show number of linked documents

- Name

- New section "Related Documents from Library" to link documents from the document_library module
- Chatter section at the bottom for full tracking and communication

The module now has full tracking of who modified records and when, displays attachment and document counts in the list view, and allows linking to documents from your document_library module.

file.content:
from odoo import models, fields, api
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import logging

_logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class MailActivityWeeklyPerformance(models.Model):
    _name = 'mail.activity.weekly.performance'
    _description = 'Weekly Activity Performance Dashboard'
    _order = 'week_start desc'

    name = fields.Char(string="Week Period", compute="_compute_name", store=True)
    week_start = fields.Date(string="Week Start Date", required=True)
    week_end = fields.Date(string="Week End Date", compute="_compute_week_end", store=True)

    employee_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'hr.employee',
        string="Employees",
        help="Employees to track (leave empty for all)"
    )

    # Week Lines (one per employee)
    weekly_line_ids = fields.One2many(
        'mail.activity.weekly.performance.line',
        'weekly_performance_id',
        string="Weekly Employee Performance",
        compute="_compute_weekly_lines",
        store=True,
        readonly=True  # Frozen after computation - prevents recalculation
    )

    is_frozen = fields.Boolean(
        string="Data Frozen",
        default=False,
        help="When True, data is locked and won't recompute"
    )

    @api.depends('week_start')
    def _compute_name(self):
        for record in self:
            if record.week_start:
                week_end = record.week_start + timedelta(days=6)
                record.name = f"Week of {record.week_start.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} to {week_end.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}"
            else:
                record.name = "New Week"

    @api.depends('week_start')
    def _compute_week_end(self):
        for record in self:
            if record.week_start:
                record.week_end = record.week_start + timedelta(days=6)
            else:
                record.week_end = False

    @api.depends('week_start', 'employee_ids')
    def _compute_weekly_lines(self):
        for record in self:
            # Don't recompute if data is frozen (week is over)
            if record.is_frozen:
                continue

            if not record.week_start:
                record.weekly_line_ids = False
                continue

            # Auto-freeze if week is over
            today = fields.Date.today()
            if record.week_end and today > record.week_end:
                record.is_frozen = True
                continue

            employees = record.employee_ids if record.employee_ids else self.env['hr.employee'].search([])

            line_vals = []
            for employee in employees:
                vals = record._compute_employee_weekly_metrics(employee)
                if vals:
                    line_vals.append((0, 0, vals))

            record.weekly_line_ids = [(5,)] + line_vals

    def _compute_employee_weekly_metrics(self, employee):
        """Compute all weekly metrics for a single employee"""
        week_start = self.week_start
        week_end = self.week_end
        prior_week_start = week_start - timedelta(days=7)
        prior_week_end = week_start - timedelta(days=1)

        # Get activities for current week
        current_week_activities = self._get_activities_for_period(
            employee, week_start, week_end
        )

        # Get activities for prior week
        prior_week_activities = self._get_activities_for_period(
            employee, prior_week_start, prior_week_end
        )

        if not current_week_activities and not prior_week_activities:
            return None

        # Calculate Prior Week Metrics
        prior_week_metrics = self._calculate_week_metrics(
            prior_week_activities, prior_week_start, prior_week_end
        )

        # Calculate Current Week Accumulated Metrics
        current_week_metrics = self._calculate_week_metrics(
            current_week_activities, week_start, week_end
        )

        # Calculate Daily Metrics (Monday to Sunday)
        daily_metrics = []
        for day_offset in range(7):
            day_date = week_start + timedelta(days=day_offset)
            day_metrics = self._calculate_daily_metrics(
                current_week_activities, day_date, day_offset
            )
            daily_metrics.append((0, 0, day_metrics))

        return {
            'employee_id': employee.id,
            # Prior Week
            'prior_week_open_vs_completed': prior_week_metrics['open_vs_completed'],
            'prior_week_p1_strikes': prior_week_metrics['p1_strikes'],
            # Week Accumulated
            'week_open_total': current_week_metrics['open_total'],
            'week_past_due': current_week_metrics['past_due'],
            'week_missing_date': current_week_metrics['missing_date'],
            'week_completed_total': current_week_metrics['completed_total'],
            'week_progress_percent': current_week_metrics['progress_percent'],
            'week_open_vs_completed': current_week_metrics['open_vs_completed'],
            'week_p1_strikes': current_week_metrics['p1_strikes'],
            # Breakdown - where activities come from
            'week_created_count': current_week_metrics['created_count'],
            'week_deadline_count': current_week_metrics['deadline_count'],
            'week_total_worked': current_week_metrics['total_worked'],
            # Daily lines
            'daily_line_ids': daily_metrics,
        }

    def _get_activities_for_period(self, employee, date_from, date_to):
        """Get all activities for an employee in a period
        Includes activities that have ANY interaction with this period:
        - Created in this period
        - Have deadline in this period
        - Were completed in this period
        """
        _logger.info(f"=== _get_activities_for_period Debug ===")

from odoo import models, fields, api


class MailActivityDepartmentKpi(models.Model):
    _name = 'mail.activity.department.kpi'
    _description = 'Mail Activity Department KPI'

    name = fields.Char(string="Period Name", compute="_compute_name", store=True)
    date_from = fields.Date(string="Date From", required=True)
    date_to = fields.Date(string="Date To", required=True)

    department_ids = fields.Many2many(
        'hr.department',
        string="Departments",
        help="Departments to analyze (leave empty for all)"
    )

    kpi_line_ids = fields.One2many(
        'mail.activity.department.kpi.line',
        'kpi_id',
        string="Department KPIs",
        compute="_compute_kpi_lines",
        store=True
    )

    @api.depends('date_from', 'date_to')
    def _compute_name(self):
        for record in self:
            if record.date_from and record.date_to:
                record.name = f"{record.date_from.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')} to {record.date_to.strftime('%Y-%m-%d')}"
            else:
                record.name = "New Period"

    @api.depends('date_from', 'date_to', 'department_ids')
    def _compute_kpi_lines(self):
        for record in self:
            if not record.date_from or not record.date_to:
                record.kpi_line_ids = False
                continue

            activities = self.env['mail.activity'].search([
                ('create_date', '>=', fields.Datetime.to_datetime(record.date_from)),
                ('create_date', '<=', fields.Datetime.to_datetime(record.date_to).replace(hour=23, minute=59, second=59))
            ])

            departments = record.department_ids if record.department_ids else self.env['hr.department'].search([])

            line_vals = []
            for dept in departments:
                dept_activities = activities.filtered(
                    lambda a: a.user_id.employee_id.department_id.id == dept.id
                )

                if len(dept_activities) == 0:
                    continue

                total_count = len(dept_activities)
                open_count = len(dept_activities.filtered(lambda a: a.custom_state == 'planned'))
                closed_count = len(dept_activities.filtered(lambda a: a.custom_state == 'done'))
                cancelled_count = len(dept_activities.filtered(lambda a: a.custom_state == 'cancelled'))

                completion_rate = (closed_count / total_count) if total_count > 0 else 0.0

                completion_days = []
                on_time_count = 0
                for activity in dept_activities.filtered(lambda a: a.custom_state == 'done'):
                    if activity.create_date and activity.completed_date:
                        delta = (activity.completed_date - activity.create_date).days
                        if delta >= 0:
                            completion_days.append(delta)

                    if activity.date_deadline and activity.completed_date:
                        completed_date_only = activity.completed_date.date()
                        if completed_date_only <= activity.date_deadline:
                            on_time_count += 1

                avg_completion_days = sum(completion_days) / len(completion_days) if completion_days else 0.0
                on_time_rate = (on_time_count / closed_count) if closed_count > 0 else 0.0

                today = fields.Date.today()
                overdue_count = len(dept_activities.filtered(
                    lambda a: a.custom_state == 'planned' and a.date_deadline and a.date_deadline < today
                ))

                postponed_count = len(dept_activities.filtered(lambda a: a.is_postponed))
                postponed_rate = (postponed_count / total_count) if total_count > 0 else 0.0

                line_vals.append((0, 0, {
                    'department_id': dept.id,
                    'total_activities': total_count,
                    'open_activities': open_count,
                    'closed_activities': closed_count,
                    'cancelled_activities': cancelled_count,
                    'completion_rate': completion_rate,
                    'avg_completion_days': avg_completion_days,
                    'on_time_completion_count': on_time_count,
                    'on_time_completion_rate': on_time_rate,
                    'overdue_count': overdue_count,
                    'postponed_count': postponed_count,
                    'postponed_rate': postponed_rate,

class MailActivityDailyPerformance(models.Model):
    _name = 'mail.activity.daily.performance'
    _description = 'Daily Activity Performance'
    _order = 'day_date'

    weekly_line_id = fields.Many2one(
        'mail.activity.weekly.performance.line',
        string="Weekly Performance Line",
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade'
    )

    day_date = fields.Date(string="Date", required=True)
    day_name = fields.Char(string="Day", required=True)

    # Daily Metrics
    open_total_day = fields.Integer(
        string="Open Total",
        help="Total open activities for this day"
    )
    past_due_day = fields.Integer(
        string="Past Due",
        help="Past due activities"
    )
    missing_date_day = fields.Integer(
        string="Missing Date",
        help="Activities without deadline"
    )
    completed_today = fields.Integer(
        string="Completed Today",
        help="Activities completed on this day"
    )
    open_vs_completed = fields.Float(
        string="Open vs Completed %",
        help="Ratio of open to completed"
    )
    new_p1_items = fields.Integer(
        string="New P1 Items",
        help="New high-priority items created today"
    )
    p1_strikes_day = fields.Integer(
        string="P1 Strikes",
        help="P1 strikes for this day"
    )

    # Daily Breakdown - where activities come from
    created_today_count = fields.Integer(
        string="Created Today",
        help="Activities created on this day"
    )

class MailActivityWeeklyDepartmentSummary(models.Model):
    _name = 'mail.activity.weekly.department.summary'
    _description = 'Weekly Department Performance Summary'
    _order = 'department_id'

    weekly_performance_id = fields.Many2one(
        'mail.activity.weekly.performance',
        string="Weekly Performance",
        required=True,
        ondelete='cascade'
    )

    department_id = fields.Many2one(
        'hr.department',
        string="Department",
        required=True
    )

    # Prior Week Summary
    prior_week_open_vs_completed = fields.Float(
        string="Prior Week Open vs Completed %",
        help="Average daily rate for prior week"
    )
    prior_week_p1_strikes = fields.Integer(
        string="Prior Week P1 Strikes",
        help="Total P1 strikes in prior week"
    )

    # Week Accumulated
    week_open_total = fields.Integer(string="Week Open Total")
    week_missing_date = fields.Integer(string="Week Missing Date")
    week_completed_total = fields.Integer(string="Week Completed Total")
    week_progress_percent = fields.Float(string="Week Progress %")
    week_p1_strikes = fields.Integer(string="Week P1 Strikes")
    week_total_worked = fields.Integer(string="Total Worked On")

if record.is_frozen:
                continue

            if not record.week_start:
                record.weekly_line_ids = False
                continue

            # Auto-freeze if week is over
            today = fields.Date.today()
            if record.week_end and today > record.week_end:
                record.is_frozen = True
                continue

            employees = record.employee_ids if record.employee_ids else self.env['hr.employee'].search([])

            line_vals = []
            for employee in employees:
                vals = record._compute_employee_weekly_metrics(employee)
                if vals:
                    line_vals.append((0, 0, vals))

            record.weekly_line_ids = [(5,)] + line_vals

    def _compute_employee_weekly_metrics(self, employee):
        """Compute all weekly metrics for a single employee"""
        week_start = self.week_start
        week_end = self.week_end
        prior_week_start = week_start - timedelta(days=7)
        prior_week_end = week_start - timedelta(days=1)

        # Get activities for current week
        current_week_activities = self._get_activities_for_period(
            employee, week_start, week_end
        )

        # Get activities for prior week
        prior_week_activities = self._get_activities_for_period(
            employee, prior_week_start, prior_week_end
        )

        if not current_week_activities and not prior_week_activities:
            return None

        # Calculate Prior Week Metrics
        prior_week_metrics = self._calculate_week_metrics(
            prior_week_activities, prior_week_start, prior_week_end
        )

        # Calculate Current Week Accumulated Metrics
        current_week_metrics = self._calculate_week_metrics(
            current_week_activities, week_start, week_end
        )

        # Calculate Daily Metrics (Monday to Sunday)
        daily_metrics = []
        for day_offset in range(7):
            day_date = week_start + timedelta(days=day_offset)
            day_metrics = self._calculate_daily_metrics(
                current_week_activities, day_date, day_offset
            )
            daily_metrics.append((0, 0, day_metrics))

        return {
            'employee_id': employee.id,
            # Prior Week
            'prior_week_open_vs_completed': prior_week_metrics['open_vs_completed'],
            'prior_week_p1_strikes': prior_week_metrics['p1_strikes'],
            # Week Accumulated
            'week_open_total': current_week_metrics['open_total'],
            'week_past_due': current_week_metrics['past_due'],
            'week_missing_date': current_week_metrics['missing_date'],
            'week_completed_total': current_week_metrics['completed_total'],
            'week_progress_percent': current_week_metrics['progress_percent'],
            'week_open_vs_completed': current_week_metrics['open_vs_completed'],
            'week_p1_strikes': current_week_metrics['p1_strikes'],
            # Breakdown - where activities come from
            'week_created_count': current_week_metrics['created_count'],
            'week_deadline_count': current_week_metrics['deadline_count'],
            'week_total_worked': current_week_metrics['total_worked'],
            # Daily lines
            'daily_line_ids': daily_metrics,
        }

    def _get_activities_for_period(self, employee, date_from, date_to):
        """Get all activities for an employee in a period
        Includes activities that have ANY interaction with this period:
        - Created in this period
        - Have deadline in this period
        - Were completed in this period
        """
        _logger.info(f"=== _get_activities_for_period Debug ===")
        _logger.info(f"Employee: {employee.name} (ID: {employee.id})")
        _logger.info(f"Period: {date_from} to {date_to}")

        # Get activities for this employee with broad date range
        # Include archived (completed) activities by disabling active_test
        all_activities = self.env['mail.activity'].with_context(active_test=False).search([
            ('user_id.employee_id', '=', employee.id),
        ])

        _logger.info(f"ORM search returned {len(all_activities)} activities")
        for act in all_activities:
            _logger.info(f"  Activity {act.id}: user_id={act.user_id.id}, "
                        f"user.employee_id={act.user_id.employee_id.id if act.user_id.employee_id else 'None'}, "
                        f"state={act.custom_state}, created={act.create_date.date() if act.create_date else None}, "
                        f"deadline={act.date_deadline}, completed={act.completed_date}")

        # Also try searching without employee filter to see all activities
        all_activities_no_filter = self.env['mail.activity'].with_context(active_test=False).search([])
        _logger.info(f"Total activities in system (no filter): {len(all_activities_no_filter)}")

        # Filter to include only activities relevant to this specific period
        relevant_activities = all_activities.filtered(
            lambda a: (
                # Created in this period
                (a.create_date and date_from <= a.create_date.date() <= date_to) or
                # Deadline in this period
                (a.date_deadline and date_from <= a.date_deadline <= date_to) or
                # Completed in this period
                (a.completed_date and date_from <= a.completed_date.date() <= date_to)

content.text:
Perfect! I've added two new summary tabs to the weekly performance dashboard. Here's what I've implemented:

**New Models Added (mail_activity_weekly_performance.py):**
1. **MailActivityWeeklyDepartmentSummary** - Aggregates metrics by department
2. **MailActivityWeeklyCompanySummary** - Aggregates metrics company-wide

**New Fields in MailActivityWeeklyPerformance:**
- `department_summary_ids` - One2many field for department summaries
- `company_summary_ids` - One2many field for company summary

**New Compute Methods:**
- `_compute_department_summary()` - Groups employees by department and aggregates:
  - Prior week metrics (open vs completed %, P1 strikes)
  - Current week totals (open, completed, progress %, P1 strikes, total worked)
  
- `_compute_company_summary()` - Aggregates all employees company-wide with:
  - Same metrics as department summary
  - Additional: total employees count, total departments count

**New View Tabs (mail_activity_weekly_performance_views.xml):**
1. **"Department Summary" tab** - Shows aggregated metrics per department with color coding:
   - Green: Progress ≥ 85%
   - Yellow: Progress 70-85%
   - Red: Progress < 70% or P1 strikes > 0

2. **"Company Summary" tab** - Shows company-wide aggregated metrics with:
   - Employee and department counts
   - Same color-coded performance indicators

Both tabs use the same metric structure as the employee dashboard for easy comparison across organizational levels.

**Summary of changes:**

**mail_activity_kpi_dashboard module:**
- ✅ Added 2 new models for department and company summaries

**company_information module:**

- ✅ Updated list view to show: notes, # attachments, library documents (as clickable tags), modified by, modified on

- ✅ Added chatter section for tracking and communication

content.text:
Perfect! I've updated the import logic with the following changes:

**1. Column Processing (line 67):**
- Changed to **only process** columns where `header_value` starts with "Total"
- Skips all other columns

**2. COGS_ROWS Mapping:**
Now organized by distinct row numbers for each category:
- **Materials**: rows 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, 19
- **Equipment**: row 13
- **Miscellaneous**: rows 14, 16, 20, 21, 22, 23
- **Freight**: row 15
- **Bond**: row 17
- **Subcontractor**: rows 18, 27, 29
- **Travel**: row 25
- **Labor**: rows 26, 28

**3. Value Extraction:**
- Each category now sums values from all its assigned rows
- Example: Materials = sum of values from rows 7, 8, 9, 10, 11, 12, and 19

**4. Updated Fields:**
- Removed the old "reserve" calculation
- Added new fields for equipment, freight, and bond
- All expense categories are now logged and saved to the project

The wizard will now only import data from "Total" columns and properly categorize expenses based on the specific row mappings you provided.

Perfect! I've updated the regex patterns to handle headers starting with "Total". The patterns now work as follows:

**Pattern 1 (with date):**
```regex
^Total\s+(\d{6})\s+(\d{1,4}(?:-\d{1,4})*)(?!\d)
```
- Matches: `"Total 021826 5393 5 Year Doc Shredding Services"`
- Extracts: 
  - Date: `021826`
  - Project number: `5393`

**Pattern 2 (fallback without date):**
```regex
^Total\s+(\d{1,4}(?:-\d{1,4})*)(?!\d)
```
- Matches: `"Total 5393 5 Year Doc Shredding Services"`
- Extracts:
  - Project number: `5393`
  - Uses today's date as fallback

The regex will now correctly parse your column title format!

message.content: just confirm to no data will duplicate if is same day

content.text:
Correct! There will be **no duplication** with the current logic. Here's how it works:

**Within the same Excel file:**
- If the same project appears multiple times, only the **latest date** is kept
- If dates are the same, the **last occurrence** in the file is kept
- Data is stored in a dictionary by project number, so duplicates are automatically overwritten

**When updating Odoo projects:**
- The code uses `project.write(values)` which **updates/overwrites** existing fields
- It does NOT create new records or append data
- Same day imports will **replace** the previous values, not duplicate them

**Example:**
1. Project 5393 has data from 2026-05-22 with Materials = $1000
2. You import Excel with same project, same date, Materials = $1500
3. Result: Project 5393 has Materials = $1500 (updated, not duplicated)

The data is **replaced**, not duplicated. Each project will only have one set of expense values at any time.

content.text:
Perfect! I've added a second pass to the import logic. Now it works like this:

**PASS 1: Process "Total" columns**
- Finds all columns with "Total" prefix
- Extracts project data
- Adds to project_updates dictionary

**PASS 2: Process non-"Total" columns (single concepts)**
- Finds columns WITHOUT "Total" prefix
- Uses regex: `(\d{6})\s+(\d{1,4}(?:-\d{1,4})*)` (without "Total")
- **Skips** if project was already found in Pass 1
- Only adds new projects not yet in the list

This ensures all projects are imported, whether they have "Total" or not!

Let me upgrade the module:

content.text:
Done! The module has been upgraded and Odoo restarted. The import wizard now uses a **two-pass system**:

**Summary:**
- ✅ **Pass 1**: Processes all columns starting with "Total"
- ✅ **Pass 2**: Processes columns WITHOUT "Total" (single concepts)
- ✅ **No duplicates**: Pass 2 skips any project already found in Pass 1
- ✅ **Same day updates**: Data from the same day will update (not skip)
- ✅ **Complete coverage**: All projects are imported, whether they have "Total" or not

The import log will clearly show which projects were found in each pass!

content: Modified several Odoo modules: added CAL Requote tracking fields to CRM, linked company_information to document_library with tracking columns, added department and company summary tabs to weekly performance dashboard, and fixed project_expenses import wizard to use two-pass logic for Total and single-concept columns with updated row mappings. All modules upgraded and Odoo restarted, ready for testing.

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <!-- Set sequence attributes (metadata / potential future use) -->
    <record id="view_project_form_tab_ordering" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.project.form.tab.ordering</field>
        <field name="model">project.project</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="project.edit_project"/>
        <field name="priority">999</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">

            <!-- 1. DELIVERABLES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='deliverables']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">10</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 7. BUDGET & SOW -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='budget_sow_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">750</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 8. CHANGE ORDERS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='change_orders']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">85</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 2. NOTES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_notes_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">20</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 3. ACTIVITIES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">30</attribute>
            </xpath>         

            <!-- 5. TEAM -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='TEAM']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">50</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 6. CONTRACT INFO -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contract_info']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">40</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 8. PROJECT CONTACTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contacts']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">80</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 10. QUALITY ASSURANCE -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='quality_assurance']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">100</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 11. CAPA -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='capa']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">110</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 12. BONDING -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bonding']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">120</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 13. INCIDENTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='incidents']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">130</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 14. LESSONS LEARNED -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='lessons_learned']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">140</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 15. MEETINGS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_meetings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">150</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 16. SUBCONTRACTORS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='subcontractors_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">160</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 17. CERTIFICATIONS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='certifications']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">170</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 18. APPOINTMENTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='appointments_page']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">180</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 19. WARRANTY -->
            <!-- <xpath expr="//page[@name='warranty']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">190</attribute>
            </xpath> -->

            <!-- 20. TECHNICIAN DAILY REPORTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='technician_daily_reports']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">200</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 21. MAIL HISTORY -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='mail_history']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">210</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 22. BD MAIL (OPPORTUNITY) -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='source_opportunity_bd_mail']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">220</attribute>
            </xpath>
            
            <!-- 9. MARKETING -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='marketing']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">800</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 4. CLOSING CHECKLIST -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='closing_checklist_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">900</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- Settings tab - push to end -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='settings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">1000</attribute>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>

    <!-- Physical DOM order (what the web client actually renders) -->
    <record id="view_project_form_tab_ordering_moves" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.project.form.tab.ordering.moves</field>
        <field name="model">project.project</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="project.edit_project"/>
        <field name="priority">100</field>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <!-- Set sequence attributes (metadata / potential future use) -->
    <record id="view_project_form_tab_ordering" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.project.form.tab.ordering</field>
        <field name="model">project.project</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="project.edit_project"/>
        <field name="priority">999</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">

            <!-- 1. NOTES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_notes_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">10</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 2. TEAM -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='TEAM']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">20</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 3. CONTRACT INFO -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contract_info']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">30</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 4. PROJECT CONTACTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contacts']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">40</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 5. BUDGET & SOW -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='budget_sow_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">50</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 6. CHANGE ORDERS (not in requested list) -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='change_orders']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">60</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 7. CERTIFICATIONS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='certifications']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">70</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 8. DELIVERABLES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='deliverables']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">80</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 9. ACTIVITIES -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">90</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 10. MAIL HISTORY -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='mail_history']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">100</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 11. BD MAIL (OPPORTUNITY) (not in requested list) -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='source_opportunity_bd_mail']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">110</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 12. MEETINGS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_meetings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">120</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 13. SUBCONTRACTORS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='subcontractors_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">130</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 14. TECHNICIAN DAILY REPORTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='technician_daily_reports']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">140</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 15. INCIDENT REPORTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='incidents']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">150</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 16. LESSONS LEARNED -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='lessons_learned']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">160</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 17. CAPA -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='capa']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">170</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 18. QA -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='quality_assurance']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">180</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 19. BONDING -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='bonding']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">190</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 20. CLOSING CHECKLIST -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='closing_checklist_tab']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">200</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 21. MARKETING -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='marketing']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">210</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 22. SETTINGS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='settings']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">220</attribute>
            </xpath>

            <!-- 23. APPOINTMENTS -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='appointments_page']" position="attributes">
                <attribute name="sequence">230</attribute>
            </xpath>

        </field>
    </record>

    <!-- Physical DOM order (what the web client actually renders) -->
    <record id="view_project_form_tab_ordering_moves" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.project.form.tab.ordering.moves</field>
        <field name="model">project.project</field>
        <field name="inherit_id" ref="project.edit_project"/>
        <field name="priority">100</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">

            <!-- Move tabs into desired physical order -->
            <xpath expr="//page[@name='deliverables']" position="after">
                <page name="project_notes_tab" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='project_notes_tab']" position="after">
                <page name="activities_table_tab" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='activities_table_tab']" position="after">
                <page name="TEAM" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='TEAM']" position="after">
                <page name="contract_info" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contract_info']" position="after">
                <page name="budget_sow_tab" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='budget_sow_tab']" position="after">
                <page name="change_orders" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='change_orders']" position="after">
                <page name="contacts" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='contacts']" position="after">
                <page name="quality_assurance" position="move"/>
            </xpath>

            <xpath expr="//page[@name='quality_assurance']" position="after">
                <page name="capa" position="move"/>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8"?>
<odoo>
    <!-- ============================================================ -->
    <!-- ACTIONS - Define these first so they can be referenced later -->
    <!-- ============================================================ -->

    <!-- Full Screen Checklist View -->
    <record id="view_kickoff_checklist_tree_fullscreen" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.kickoff.item.tree.fullscreen</field>
        <field name="model">project.kickoff.item</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <list editable="bottom" multi_edit="1">
                <field name="sequence" widget="handle"/>
                <field name="name" required="1"/>
                <field name="description" optional="hide"/>
                <field name="date_required" optional="show"/>
                <field name="status" widget="badge"
                       decoration-success="status == 'completed'"
                       decoration-info="status == 'in_progress'"
                       decoration-warning="status == 'on_hold'"
                       decoration-muted="status == 'not_applicable'"/>
                <field name="attachment_ids" widget="many2many_binary" optional="hide"/>
                <field name="acg_txt" widget="html" optional="show"/>
                <field name="customer_txt" widget="html" optional="show"/>
                <field name="subcontractor_txt" widget="html" optional="show"/>
            </list>
        </field>
    </record>

    <record id="view_kickoff_checklist_form_fullscreen" model="ir.ui.view">
        <field name="name">project.kickoff.item.form.fullscreen</field>
        <field name="model">project.kickoff.item</field>
        <field name="arch" type="xml">
            <form>
                <sheet>
                    <group>
                        <group>
                            <field name="name"/>
                            <field name="date_required"/>
                            <field name="status"/>
                        </group>
                        <group>
                            <field name="sequence"/>
                            <field name="attachment_ids" widget="many2many_binary"/>
                        </group>
                    </group>
                    <notebook>
                        <page string="ACG Response">
                            <field name="acg_txt" widget="html" options="{'height': 400}"/>
                        </page>

from odoo import models, fields, api
from odoo.exceptions import UserError
from datetime import date


class ProjectDaily(models.Model):
    _name = 'project.daily'
    _description = 'Project Daily Report'
    _order = 'date desc, id desc'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']

    name = fields.Char(string='Reference', required=True, copy=False, readonly=True, default='New')
    project_id = fields.Many2one('project.project', string='Project', required=True, tracking=True)
    employee_id = fields.Many2one('hr.employee', string='Technician', required=True, tracking=True,
                                   default=lambda self: self.env.user.employee_id)
    date = fields.Date(string='Date', required=True, default=fields.Date.context_today, tracking=True)

    description = fields.Text(string='Work Description', required=True)
    hours_spent = fields.Float(string='Hours Spent')
    materials_used = fields.Text(string='Materials Used')
    issues = fields.Text(string='Issues/Problems Encountered')
    location = fields.Char(string='Location/Area')
    weather = fields.Selection([
        ('sunny', 'Sunny'),
        ('cloudy', 'Cloudy'),
        ('rainy', 'Rainy'),
        ('stormy', 'Stormy'),
        ('snowy', 'Snowy'),
        ('windy', 'Windy'),
    ], string='Weather Conditions')

    image_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Images')
    image_count = fields.Integer(string='Image Count', compute='_compute_image_count')

    receipt_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Receipts',
                                   domain=[('image_type', '=', 'receipt')])
    receipt_count = fields.Integer(string='Receipt Count', compute='_compute_receipt_count')

    timesheet_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.attachment', 'daily_id', string='Timesheets')
    timesheet_count = fields.Integer(string='Timesheet Count', compute='_compute_timesheet_count')

    company_id = fields.Many2one('res.company', string='Company',
                                  default=lambda self: self.env.company)

    # Configuration fields for automatic activities (can be set via Settings)
    scheduling_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Scheduling User',
                                         help='User to assign scheduling tasks (Elena or Alejandra)')
    pm_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Project Manager',
                                  related='project_id.user_id', store=False,
                                  help='Project Manager from the project')

    @api.depends('image_ids')
    def _compute_image_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.image_count = len(record.image_ids.filtered(lambda img: img.image_type == 'photo'))

    @api.depends('receipt_ids')
    def _compute_receipt_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.receipt_count = len(record.receipt_ids)

    @api.depends('timesheet_ids')
    def _compute_timesheet_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.timesheet_count = len(record.timesheet_ids)

    @api.model_create_multi
    def create(self, vals_list):
        for vals in vals_list:
            if not vals.get('name') or vals.get('name') == 'New':
                sequence = self.env['ir.sequence'].sudo().next_by_code('project.daily')
                if not sequence:
                    raise UserError('Sequence for Project Daily Report not found. Please contact administrator.')
                vals['name'] = sequence

        records = super().create(vals_list)

        # Create automatic activities for each daily report
        for record in records:
            record._create_automatic_activities()

        return records

    def _create_automatic_activities(self):
        """Create automatic activities when a daily report is submitted"""
        self.ensure_one()

        # Get scheduling user from company config (stored in system parameters)
        scheduling_user = self.env.company.get_daily_scheduling_user()
        pm_user = self.project_id.user_id

        activity_type = self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_data_todo', raise_if_not_found=False)
        if not activity_type:
            return

        # Task 1: Create Timesheet (assigned to scheduling user)
        if scheduling_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Create Timesheet',

from odoo import models, fields, api
from odoo.exceptions import UserError
from datetime import date


class ProjectDaily(models.Model):
    _name = 'project.daily'
    _description = 'Project Daily Report'
    _order = 'date desc, id desc'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']

    name = fields.Char(string='Reference', required=True, copy=False, readonly=True, default='New')
    project_id = fields.Many2one('project.project', string='Project', required=True, tracking=True)
    employee_id = fields.Many2one('hr.employee', string='Technician', required=True, tracking=True,
                                   default=lambda self: self.env.user.employee_id)
    date = fields.Date(string='Date', required=True, default=fields.Date.context_today, tracking=True)

    description = fields.Text(string='Work Description', required=True)
    hours_spent = fields.Float(string='Hours Spent')
    materials_used = fields.Text(string='Materials Used')
    issues = fields.Text(string='Issues/Problems Encountered')
    location = fields.Char(string='Location/Area')
    weather = fields.Selection([
        ('sunny', 'Sunny'),
        ('cloudy', 'Cloudy'),
        ('rainy', 'Rainy'),
        ('stormy', 'Stormy'),
        ('snowy', 'Snowy'),
        ('windy', 'Windy'),
    ], string='Weather Conditions')

    image_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Images')
    image_count = fields.Integer(string='Image Count', compute='_compute_image_count')

    receipt_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Receipts',
                                   domain=[('image_type', '=', 'receipt')])
    receipt_count = fields.Integer(string='Receipt Count', compute='_compute_receipt_count')

    timesheet_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.attachment', 'daily_id', string='Timesheets')
    timesheet_count = fields.Integer(string='Timesheet Count', compute='_compute_timesheet_count')

    company_id = fields.Many2one('res.company', string='Company',
                                  default=lambda self: self.env.company)

    # Configuration fields for automatic activities (can be set via Settings)
    scheduling_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Scheduling User',
                                         help='User to assign scheduling tasks (Elena or Alejandra)')
    pm_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Project Manager',
                                  related='project_id.user_id', store=False,
                                  help='Project Manager from the project')

    @api.depends('image_ids')
    def _compute_image_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.image_count = len(record.image_ids.filtered(lambda img: img.image_type == 'photo'))

    @api.depends('receipt_ids')
    def _compute_receipt_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.receipt_count = len(record.receipt_ids)

    @api.depends('timesheet_ids')
    def _compute_timesheet_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.timesheet_count = len(record.timesheet_ids)

    @api.model_create_multi
    def create(self, vals_list):
        for vals in vals_list:
            if not vals.get('name') or vals.get('name') == 'New':
                sequence = self.env['ir.sequence'].sudo().next_by_code('project.daily')
                if not sequence:
                    raise UserError('Sequence for Project Daily Report not found. Please contact administrator.')
                vals['name'] = sequence

        records = super().create(vals_list)

        # Create automatic activities for each daily report
        for record in records:
            record._create_automatic_activities()

        return records

    def _create_automatic_activities(self):
        """Create automatic activities when a daily report is submitted"""
        self.ensure_one()

        # Get scheduling user from company config (stored in system parameters)
        scheduling_user = self.env.company.get_daily_scheduling_user()
        pm_user = self.project_id.user_id

        activity_type = self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_data_todo', raise_if_not_found=False)
        if not activity_type:
            return

        # Task 1: Create Timesheet (assigned to scheduling user)
        if scheduling_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Create Timesheet',
                note=f'Create timesheet for daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=scheduling_user.id
            )

        # Task 2: Upload Timesheet (assigned to scheduling user)
        if scheduling_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Upload Timesheet',
                note=f'Upload timesheet for daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=scheduling_user.id
            )

        # Task 3: Project Manager Authorization (assigned to PM)
        if pm_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Project Manager Authorization',
                note=f'Review and authorize daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=pm_user.id
            )

    def write(self, vals):
        for record in self:
            if record.date != date.today() and not self.env.user.has_group('project.group_project_manager'):
                raise UserError('You can only edit daily reports created today.')
        return super().write(vals)

    def unlink(self):
        if not self.env.user.has_group('project_dailys.group_project_daily_manager'):
            raise UserError('Only managers can delete daily reports.')
        return super().unlink()

    def action_view_images(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Photos',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.image',
            'view_mode': 'kanban,list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id), ('image_type', '=', 'photo')],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id, 'default_image_type': 'photo'},
        }

    def action_view_receipts(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Receipts',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.image',
            'view_mode': 'kanban,list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id), ('image_type', '=', 'receipt')],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id, 'default_image_type': 'receipt'},
        }

    def action_view_timesheets(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Timesheets',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.attachment',
            'view_mode': 'list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id)],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id},
        }

content.text:
Done! The project_dailys module has been updated and Odoo restarted. Here's what changed:

**Model Changes (project_daily.py):**
- ✅ Hidden "Hours Spent" (kept for backward compatibility)
- ✅ Added **Time In** - Float field with float_time widget (e.g., 8.5 = 8:30 AM)
- ✅ Added **Time Out** - Float field with float_time widget (e.g., 17.5 = 5:30 PM)
- ✅ Added **Lunch Time** - Float field in hours with float_time widget
- ✅ Added **How many techs on site** - Integer field (default: 1)

**Form View Changes:**
- ✅ "Hours Spent" is now invisible
- ✅ Added "Time Tracking" section with Time In, Time Out, and Lunch Time
- ✅ Added "Team" section with techs on site counter

**List View Changes:**
- ✅ Shows new time tracking fields (Time In, Time Out, Lunch Time)
- ✅ Shows techs on site count
- ✅ "Hours Spent" is hidden by default (optional="hide" - users can show it if needed)

All fields use the `float_time` widget which displays time in HH:MM format (e.g., 8:30, 17:30) for better readability!

    def daily_submit(self, **post):
        try:
            project_id = int(post.get('project_id'))
            employee_id = int(post.get('employee_id'))
            date = post.get('date')
            description = post.get('description')

            materials_used = post.get('materials_used', '')
            issues = post.get('issues', '')
            location = post.get('location', '')
            weather = post.get('weather', '')

def _time_to_float(self, time_str):
        """Convert HH:MM time string to float (e.g., '08:30' -> 8.5)"""
        if not time_str:
            return 0.0
        try:
            hours, minutes = time_str.split(':')
            return int(hours) + int(minutes) / 60.0
        except:
            return 0.0

    @http.route('/project/daily/submit', type='json', auth='user', methods=['POST'])
    def daily_submit(self, **post):
        try:
            project_id = int(post.get('project_id'))
            employee_id = int(post.get('employee_id'))
            date = post.get('date')
            description = post.get('description')
            materials_used = post.get('materials_used', '')
            issues = post.get('issues', '')
            location = post.get('location', '')
            weather = post.get('weather', '')

            # New time tracking fields
            time_in = self._time_to_float(post.get('time_in', ''))
            time_out = self._time_to_float(post.get('time_out', ''))
            lunch_time = float(post.get('lunch_time', 0))
            techs_on_site = int(post.get('techs_on_site', 1))

            images = post.get('images', [])
            receipts = post.get('receipts', [])
            timesheets = post.get('timesheets', [])

            daily = request.env['project.daily'].create({
                'project_id': project_id,
                'employee_id': employee_id,
                'date': date,
                'description': description,

Perfect! All changes have been applied and Odoo has been restarted. Here's a complete summary of what was updated:

**1. Model (project_daily.py):**
- ✅ Hidden `hours_spent` field
- ✅ Added `time_in` (Float - time clock in)
- ✅ Added `time_out` (Float - time clock out)
- ✅ Added `lunch_time` (Float - lunch duration in hours)
- ✅ Added `techs_on_site` (Integer - number of technicians, default: 1)

**2. Form View (project_daily_views.xml):**
- ✅ Made `hours_spent` invisible
- ✅ Added "Time Tracking" group with Time In/Out/Lunch fields using `float_time` widget
- ✅ Added "Team" group with techs counter

**3. List View (project_daily_views.xml):**
- ✅ Shows all new time tracking fields
- ✅ Shows techs on site count
- ✅ Made `hours_spent` optional hide (hidden by default)

**4. Web Form Template (daily_form_template.xml):**
- ✅ Replaced hours_spent with new time tracking fields
- ✅ Time In/Out use HTML5 `time` input (HH:MM format)
- ✅ Lunch time uses number input with 0.25 step
- ✅ Techs on site with default value of 1

**5. Controller (main.py):**
- ✅ Added `_time_to_float()` method to convert HH:MM to decimal (8:30 → 8.5)
- ✅ Updated submit handler to process new fields
- ✅ Removed old hours_spent processing

**Now please refresh your browser (Ctrl+Shift+R) to see all the changes!**

from odoo import models, fields, api
from odoo.exceptions import UserError
from datetime import date


class ProjectDaily(models.Model):
    _name = 'project.daily'
    _description = 'Project Daily Report'
    _order = 'date desc, id desc'
    _inherit = ['mail.thread', 'mail.activity.mixin']

    name = fields.Char(string='Reference', required=True, copy=False, readonly=True, default='New')
    project_id = fields.Many2one('project.project', string='Project', required=True, tracking=True)
    employee_id = fields.Many2one('hr.employee', string='Technician', required=True, tracking=True,
                                   default=lambda self: self.env.user.employee_id)
    date = fields.Date(string='Date', required=True, default=fields.Date.context_today, tracking=True)

    description = fields.Text(string='Work Description', required=True)
    hours_spent = fields.Float(string='Hours Spent')  # Hidden - kept for backward compatibility

    # New time tracking fields
    time_in = fields.Float(string='Time In', help='Time clock in (e.g., 8.5 for 8:30 AM)')
    time_out = fields.Float(string='Time Out', help='Time clock out (e.g., 17.5 for 5:30 PM)')
    lunch_time = fields.Float(string='Lunch Time (hours)', help='Duration of lunch break in hours')
    techs_on_site = fields.Integer(string='How many techs on site', default=1)

    materials_used = fields.Text(string='Materials Used')
    issues = fields.Text(string='Issues/Problems Encountered')
    location = fields.Char(string='Location/Area')
    weather = fields.Selection([
        ('sunny', 'Sunny'),
        ('cloudy', 'Cloudy'),
        ('rainy', 'Rainy'),
        ('stormy', 'Stormy'),
        ('snowy', 'Snowy'),
        ('windy', 'Windy'),
    ], string='Weather Conditions')

    image_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Images')
    image_count = fields.Integer(string='Image Count', compute='_compute_image_count')

    receipt_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.image', 'daily_id', string='Receipts',
                                   domain=[('image_type', '=', 'receipt')])
    receipt_count = fields.Integer(string='Receipt Count', compute='_compute_receipt_count')

    timesheet_ids = fields.One2many('project.daily.attachment', 'daily_id', string='Timesheets')
    timesheet_count = fields.Integer(string='Timesheet Count', compute='_compute_timesheet_count')

    company_id = fields.Many2one('res.company', string='Company',
                                  default=lambda self: self.env.company)

    # Configuration fields for automatic activities (can be set via Settings)
    scheduling_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Scheduling User',
                                         help='User to assign scheduling tasks (Elena or Alejandra)')
    pm_user_id = fields.Many2one('res.users', string='Project Manager',
                                  related='project_id.user_id', store=False,
                                  help='Project Manager from the project')

    @api.depends('image_ids')
    def _compute_image_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.image_count = len(record.image_ids.filtered(lambda img: img.image_type == 'photo'))

    @api.depends('receipt_ids')
    def _compute_receipt_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.receipt_count = len(record.receipt_ids)

    @api.depends('timesheet_ids')
    def _compute_timesheet_count(self):
        for record in self:
            record.timesheet_count = len(record.timesheet_ids)

    @api.model_create_multi
    def create(self, vals_list):
        for vals in vals_list:
            if not vals.get('name') or vals.get('name') == 'New':
                sequence = self.env['ir.sequence'].sudo().next_by_code('project.daily')
                if not sequence:
                    raise UserError('Sequence for Project Daily Report not found. Please contact administrator.')
                vals['name'] = sequence

        records = super().create(vals_list)

        # Create automatic activities for each daily report
        for record in records:
            record._create_automatic_activities()

        return records

    def _create_automatic_activities(self):
        """Create automatic activities when a daily report is submitted"""
        self.ensure_one()

        # Get scheduling user from company config (stored in system parameters)
        scheduling_user = self.env.company.get_daily_scheduling_user()
        pm_user = self.project_id.user_id

        activity_type = self.env.ref('mail.mail_activity_data_todo', raise_if_not_found=False)
        if not activity_type:
            return

        # Task 1: Create Timesheet (assigned to scheduling user)
        if scheduling_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Create Timesheet',
                note=f'Create timesheet for daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=scheduling_user.id
            )

        # Task 2: Upload Timesheet (assigned to scheduling user)
        if scheduling_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Upload Timesheet',
                note=f'Upload timesheet for daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=scheduling_user.id
            )

        # Task 3: Project Manager Authorization (assigned to PM)
        if pm_user:
            self.activity_schedule(
                activity_type_id=activity_type.id,
                summary='Project Manager Authorization',
                note=f'Review and authorize daily report {self.name}',
                user_id=pm_user.id
            )

    def write(self, vals):
        for record in self:
            if record.date != date.today() and not self.env.user.has_group('project.group_project_manager'):
                raise UserError('You can only edit daily reports created today.')
        return super().write(vals)

    def unlink(self):
        if not self.env.user.has_group('project_dailys.group_project_daily_manager'):
            raise UserError('Only managers can delete daily reports.')
        return super().unlink()

    def action_view_images(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Photos',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.image',
            'view_mode': 'kanban,list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id), ('image_type', '=', 'photo')],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id, 'default_image_type': 'photo'},
        }

    def action_view_receipts(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Receipts',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.image',
            'view_mode': 'kanban,list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id), ('image_type', '=', 'receipt')],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id, 'default_image_type': 'receipt'},
        }

    def action_view_timesheets(self):
        self.ensure_one()
        return {
            'name': 'Daily Report Timesheets',
            'type': 'ir.actions.act_window',
            'res_model': 'project.daily.attachment',
            'view_mode': 'list,form',
            'domain': [('daily_id', '=', self.id)],
            'context': {'default_daily_id': self.id},
        }
